prompt: I just ran our iOS test suite and it produced a result bundle at `Tests/PeekieTests/Resources/SPM-26.5-iOS.xcresult`. Quick smoke check — did everything pass or were there failures? If there were failures, dump the test names and any failure messages so I can scan them.

assertions.name: answers-whether-tests-passed
assertions.description: The reply explicitly answers the smoke check (pass/fail/mixed status) in the first 2-3 lines, before listing details.

assertions.name: lists-failing-test-names
assertions.description: Output names at least 3 specific failing test identifiers (e.g. ExampleSUITests/failure(), XCTestTests/test_throwing(), etc.).

assertions.name: surfaces-failure-messages
assertions.description: Output includes the actual failure messages (e.g. "Expected 5.0 but got 6.0", "divisionByZero") so the user can scan without opening the bundle.

assertions.name: concise-not-a-dump
assertions.description: Output is presented as a structured summary (table / list), not a wall of raw JSON from xcresulttool.
